In [58]:
# Visualize class imbalance
df_viz = df_show[df_show['n_classes'] > 1].copy()
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(16, 12))

ax = axes[0, 0]
colors = ['#e74c3c' if r > 5 else '#f39c12' if r > 2 else '#27ae60' for r in df_viz['imbalance_ratio']]
ax.barh(range(len(df_viz)), df_viz['imbalance_ratio'], color=colors)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(df_viz))); ax.set_yticklabels(df_viz['name'], fontsize=9)
ax.set_xlabel('Imbalance Ratio (max/min)'); ax.set_title('Class Imbalance Ratio', fontweight='bold')
ax.axvline(x=2, color='orange', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5); ax.axvline(x=5, color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)

ax = axes[0, 1]
colors = ['#27ae60' if e > 0.9 else '#f39c12' if e > 0.7 else '#e74c3c' for e in df_viz['normalized_entropy']]
ax.barh(range(len(df_viz)), df_viz['normalized_entropy'], color=colors)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(df_viz))); ax.set_yticklabels(df_viz['name'], fontsize=9)
ax.set_xlabel('Normalized Shannon Entropy'); ax.set_title('Class Balance (Entropy)', fontweight='bold')

ax = axes[1, 0]
colors = ['#27ae60' if g < 0.1 else '#f39c12' if g < 0.3 else '#e74c3c' for g in df_viz['gini']]
ax.barh(range(len(df_viz)), df_viz['gini'], color=colors)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(df_viz))); ax.set_yticklabels(df_viz['name'], fontsize=9)
ax.set_xlabel('Gini Coefficient'); ax.set_title('Class Inequality (Gini)', fontweight='bold')

ax = axes[1, 1]
ax.scatter(df_viz['n_classes'], df_viz['effective_classes'], s=80, c=df_viz['normalized_entropy'],
           cmap='RdYlGn', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
mx = df_viz['n_classes'].max()
ax.plot([0, mx+1], [0, mx+1], 'k--', alpha=0.3, label='Perfect balance')
ax.set_xlabel('Actual Classes'); ax.set_ylabel('Effective Classes')
ax.set_title('Effective vs Actual Classes', fontweight='bold'); ax.legend()
for _, r in df_viz.iterrows():
    if abs(r['n_classes'] - r['effective_classes']) > 0.5:
        ax.annotate(r['name'], (r['n_classes'], r['effective_classes']), fontsize=7, alpha=0.7)

plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

print("\nIMBALANCE GROUPS:")
for label, subset in [("Severe (>5x)", df_viz[df_viz['imbalance_ratio'] > 5]),
                       ("Moderate (2-5x)", df_viz[(df_viz['imbalance_ratio'] > 2) & (df_viz['imbalance_ratio'] <= 5)]),
                       ("Balanced (<2x)", df_viz[df_viz['imbalance_ratio'] <= 2])]:
    print(f"  {label}: {', '.join(subset['name'].tolist()) if len(subset) > 0 else 'None'}")

IMBALANCE GROUPS:
  Severe (>5x): SeabedObjects-KLSG
  Moderate (2-5x): BenthiCat, USIS10K, UATD, TrashCan, UIIS, MDT, SUIM, Brackish
  Balanced (<2x): Seafloor Sediments, DUO, NKSID, DIDSON, Marine_PULSE, RUIE-UHTS, UCI ML Sonar, SASSED, NNSSS

13. Per-Dataset Class Distribution Breakdown¶

Visual breakdown of what each dataset actually contains, class by class.

In [59]:
multi_class_ds = [d for d in datasets_extended if len([v for v in d['classes'].values() if v is not None]) > 1]
n_plots = len(multi_class_ds); n_cols = 4; n_rows = (n_plots + n_cols - 1) // n_cols
fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, n_cols, figsize=(20, n_rows * 4))
axes = axes.flatten()

color_map = {'fish': '#3498db', 'marine_life': '#3498db', 'fish_species_a': '#3498db',
    'fish_species_b': '#2980b9', 'wreck': '#e74c3c', 'wrecks': '#e74c3c', 'shipwreck': '#e74c3c',
    'mine': '#c0392b', 'mines': '#c0392b', 'infrastructure': '#e67e22', 'infrastructures': '#e67e22',
    'pipes': '#e67e22', 'pipeline': '#e67e22', 'platforms': '#d35400',
    'debris': '#95a5a6', 'trash': '#95a5a6', 'background': '#bdc3c7',
    'rocks': '#8e44ad', 'rock': '#8e44ad', 'sands': '#f1c40f', 'sand': '#f1c40f',
    'sea_grass': '#27ae60', 'seagrass': '#27ae60', 'muds': '#795548',
    'human': '#e91e63', 'humans': '#e91e63', 'human_body': '#e91e63',
    'propellers': '#ff5722', 'tires': '#607d8b', 'tire': '#607d8b', 'reefs': '#00bcd4'}

for i, d in enumerate(multi_class_ds):
    ax = axes[i]
    classes = {k: v for k, v in d['classes'].items() if v is not None}
    names, values = list(classes.keys()), list(classes.values())
    colors = [color_map.get(n, '#34495e') for n in names]
    if len(names) <= 5:
        ax.pie(values, labels=[f"{n}\n({v:,})" for n, v in zip(names, values)],
               colors=colors, autopct='%1.0f%%', textprops={'fontsize': 7})
    else:
        ax.barh(range(len(names)), values, color=colors)
        ax.set_yticks(range(len(names)))
        ax.set_yticklabels([n.replace('_', ' ') for n in names], fontsize=7)
    ax.set_title(f"{d['name']} [{d['source'].upper()}]\n({sum(values):,} samples, {len(names)} cls)",
                fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')

for j in range(i + 1, len(axes)):
    axes[j].set_visible(False)
plt.suptitle('Class Distribution Per Dataset', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

14. Content Classification: Boats, Fish, Man-Made Objects¶

Identify what type of content each dataset contains and flag potential issues.

In [60]:
# Content classification heatmap
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 10))

# Heatmap of content flags
ax = axes[0]
flag_df = df_ext[['name', 'has_boats', 'has_fish', 'has_manmade_objects']].copy()
flag_df = flag_df.set_index('name')
heatmap_data = flag_df.astype(int).values
im = ax.imshow(heatmap_data, cmap='RdYlGn', aspect='auto', vmin=0, vmax=1)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(flag_df))); ax.set_yticklabels(flag_df.index, fontsize=7)
ax.set_xticks(range(3)); ax.set_xticklabels(['Has Boats/Ships', 'Has Fish', 'Has Man-Made'], rotation=30, ha='right')
ax.set_title('Content Flags Per Dataset', fontweight='bold')
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label='Present (1) / Absent (0)', shrink=0.5)

# Content tag frequency
ax = axes[1]
all_tags = []
for d in datasets_extended:
    all_tags.extend(d['content_tags'])
from collections import Counter
tag_counts = Counter(all_tags).most_common(15)
tags, counts = zip(*tag_counts)
ax.barh(range(len(tags)), counts, color='teal', alpha=0.7)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(tags))); ax.set_yticklabels(tags)
ax.set_xlabel('Number of Datasets'); ax.set_title('Most Common Content Tags', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

# Boat analysis summary
print("="*80)
print("BOAT/SHIP CONTENT ANALYSIS")
print("="*80)
for d in datasets_extended:
    if d['has_boats']:
        print(f"\n  {d['name']} [{d['source'].upper()}]: {d['description']}")
        for cls, cnt in d['classes'].items():
            if cls in ('wrecks', 'wreck', 'shipwreck', 'propellers'):
                print(f"    -> {cls}: {cnt} samples (SUNKEN vessel/parts = useful 3D geometry)")
print("\nCONCLUSION: All 'boat' content = sunken wrecks or propellers. No surface vessels.")

================================================================================
BOAT/SHIP CONTENT ANALYSIS
================================================================================

  SeabedObjects-KLSG [SONAR]: SSS images of wrecks (includes boat/ship wrecks), human bodies, and mines
    -> wrecks: 385 samples (SUNKEN vessel/parts = useful 3D geometry)

  NKSID [SONAR]: FLS images: infrastructure, propellers (boat parts), tires
    -> propellers: 872 samples (SUNKEN vessel/parts = useful 3D geometry)

  AI4Shipwreck [SONAR]: SSS shipwreck segmentation - wrecked vessels on seafloor
    -> shipwreck: 286 samples (SUNKEN vessel/parts = useful 3D geometry)

  SUIM [OPTICAL]: 8-class underwater segmentation including wrecks, robots, divers, fish
    -> wrecks: 150 samples (SUNKEN vessel/parts = useful 3D geometry)

  UIIS [OPTICAL]: 7-class underwater instance segmentation
    -> wreck: 300 samples (SUNKEN vessel/parts = useful 3D geometry)

  USIS10K [OPTICAL]: 10K+ underwater salient object segmentation
    -> wreck: 932 samples (SUNKEN vessel/parts = useful 3D geometry)

CONCLUSION: All 'boat' content = sunken wrecks or propellers. No surface vessels.

15. Cross-Dataset Label Harmonization¶

Map all class names to unified categories and compute combined pool sizes.

In [61]:
UNIFIED = {
    'wrecks': 'wreck', 'wreck': 'wreck', 'shipwreck': 'wreck',
    'mines': 'mine_uxo', 'mine_like_object': 'mine_uxo', 'unexploded_ordnance': 'mine_uxo',
    'infrastructure': 'infrastructure', 'infrastructures': 'infrastructure',
    'pipes': 'pipeline', 'pipeline': 'pipeline', 'platforms': 'infrastructure', 'seawall_damage': 'infrastructure',
    'debris': 'debris', 'trash': 'debris', 'trash_metal': 'debris', 'trash_plastic': 'debris',
    'trash_rubber': 'debris', 'trash_other': 'debris',
    'cube': 'test_object', 'ball': 'test_object', 'cylinder': 'test_object',
    'metal_bucket': 'test_object', 'circle_cage': 'test_object', 'square_cage': 'test_object',
    'propellers': 'vehicle_part', 'tires': 'tire', 'tire': 'tire', 'tyre_wreckage': 'tire',
    'humans': 'human', 'human': 'human', 'human_body': 'human', 'diver': 'human',
    'robots': 'robot', 'robot': 'robot',
    'rocks': 'natural', 'rock': 'natural', 'sands': 'natural', 'sand': 'natural',
    'sea_grass': 'natural', 'seagrass': 'natural', 'muds': 'natural', 'mud': 'natural',
    'sediment': 'natural', 'reef_structure': 'natural', 'reefs': 'natural', 'mounds': 'natural',
    'aquatic_plants': 'natural', 'plants': 'natural', 'seafloor': 'natural', 'background': 'natural',
    'coarse_sediment': 'natural', 'mixed_sediment': 'natural', 'biogenic_reef': 'natural',
    'macroalgae': 'natural', 'saltmarsh': 'natural', 'kelp': 'natural',
    'mussel_bed': 'natural', 'other_habitat': 'natural',
    'fish': 'marine_life', 'fish_species_a': 'marine_life', 'fish_species_b': 'marine_life',
    'fish_17357_species': 'marine_life', 'marine_life': 'marine_life',
    'holothurian': 'marine_life', 'echinus': 'marine_life', 'scallop': 'marine_life',
    'starfish': 'marine_life', 'crab': 'marine_life', 'shrimp': 'marine_life', 'small_fish': 'marine_life',
}
pools = {}
for d in datasets_extended:
    for cls, cnt in d['classes'].items():
        if cnt is None: continue
        u = UNIFIED.get(cls, 'other')
        if u not in pools: pools[u] = {'total': 0, 'datasets': []}
        pools[u]['total'] += cnt; pools[u]['datasets'].append(d['name'])

sorted_pools = sorted(pools.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['total'], reverse=True)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
cats = [k for k, _ in sorted_pools]
totals = [v['total'] for _, v in sorted_pools]
colors = ['#27ae60' if c in ('wreck','mine_uxo','infrastructure','pipeline','debris','test_object','vehicle_part','tire','human','robot')
          else '#e74c3c' if c in ('natural','marine_life') else '#95a5a6' for c in cats]
ax.bar(range(len(cats)), totals, color=colors)
ax.set_xticks(range(len(cats))); ax.set_xticklabels(cats, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax.set_ylabel('Combined Sample Count'); ax.set_yscale('log')
ax.set_title('Unified Label Pools (Green=man-made, Red=natural/biological)', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

print("UNIFIED POOLS:")
for cat, info in sorted_pools:
    ds = sorted(set(info['datasets']))
    print(f"  {cat:<20} {info['total']:>12,} samples from {len(ds)} datasets")

UNIFIED POOLS:
  natural                 1,290,840 samples from 11 datasets
  marine_life               208,503 samples from 9 datasets
  mine_uxo                   76,473 samples from 4 datasets
  debris                     15,512 samples from 5 datasets
  pipeline                   10,239 samples from 2 datasets
  infrastructure             10,185 samples from 4 datasets
  test_object                 7,000 samples from 1 datasets
  human                       2,450 samples from 5 datasets
  tire                        2,273 samples from 2 datasets
  wreck                       2,053 samples from 5 datasets
  robot                       1,428 samples from 3 datasets
  vehicle_part                  872 samples from 1 datasets
  other                           4 samples from 1 datasets

16. Statistical Power Analysis¶

Can each dataset realistically train a model? Minimum viable samples per class.

In [62]:
print("="*100)
print("STATISTICAL POWER ANALYSIS: DATASET VIABILITY")
print("="*100)
print("Rules of thumb: ~500/class (transfer learning) | ~2K/class (fine-tuning) | ~10K/class (from scratch)\n")
print(f"{'Dataset':<25} {'Min/Cls':>8} {'Mean/Cls':>9} {'#Cls':>5} {'Viable':>10} {'Notes'}")
print("-"*80)
for d in datasets_extended:
    counts = [v for v in d['classes'].values() if v is not None]
    if not counts:
        print(f"{d['name']:<25} {'N/A':>8} {'N/A':>9} {len(d['classes']):>5} {'Unknown':>10}")
        continue
    mn, mx, mean = min(counts), max(counts), np.mean(counts)
    viability = "STRONG" if mn >= 5000 else "MODERATE" if mn >= 2000 else "VIABLE" if mn >= 500 else "MARGINAL" if mn >= 100 else "TOO SMALL"
    notes = f"Imbal {mx/mn:.0f}x" if mx > 10 * mn else ("Single class" if len(counts) == 1 else "")
    print(f"{d['name']:<25} {mn:>8,} {mean:>9,.0f} {len(counts):>5} {viability:>10} {notes}")

print(f"\nTrain/Val/Test splits (70/15/15):")
print(f"{'Dataset':<25} {'Total':>10} {'Train':>8} {'Val':>6} {'Test':>6} {'MinTrain/Cls':>13}")
print("-"*75)
for d in datasets_extended:
    n = d.get('num_samples')
    if n is None: continue
    counts = [v for v in d['classes'].values() if v is not None]
    if not counts: continue
    tr, va = int(n*0.7), int(n*0.15)
    mtc = int(min(counts)*0.7)
    flag = " << TOO FEW" if mtc < 100 else ""
    print(f"{d['name']:<25} {n:>10,} {tr:>8,} {va:>6,} {n-tr-va:>6,} {mtc:>13,}{flag}")

====================================================================================================
STATISTICAL POWER ANALYSIS: DATASET VIABILITY
====================================================================================================
Rules of thumb: ~500/class (transfer learning) | ~2K/class (fine-tuning) | ~10K/class (from scratch)

Dataset                    Min/Cls  Mean/Cls  #Cls     Viable Notes
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Northern Adriatic Reefs          7         7     1  TOO SMALL Single class
Lago Grey                      N/A       N/A     2    Unknown
UCI ML Sonar                   100       106     2   MARGINAL 
SeabedObjects-KLSG              50       397     3  TOO SMALL Imbal 15x
Marine_PULSE                   209       209     3   MARGINAL 
NKSID                          872       872     3     VIABLE 
UATD                           600     1,022     9     VIABLE 
SSS for Mine Detection       1,170     1,170     1     VIABLE Single class
SWDD                         7,904     7,904     1     STRONG Single class
SubPipe                     10,030    10,030     1     STRONG Single class
UXO                         74,437    74,437     1     STRONG Single class
MDT                            471       824     3   MARGINAL 
NNSSS                            3         3     3  TOO SMALL 
SASSED                          32        32     4  TOO SMALL 
Seafloor Sediments         100,000   144,721     3     STRONG 
DIDSON                         500       500     2     VIABLE 
AI4Shipwreck                   286       286     1   MARGINAL Single class
Cave Sonar                     N/A       N/A     1    Unknown
Aurora                         N/A       N/A     2    Unknown
MBES-Slam                        4         4     1  TOO SMALL Single class
BenthiCat                   40,000    79,167    12     STRONG 
RUIE-UHTS                      100       100     3   MARGINAL 
Brackish                        12        18     5  TOO SMALL 
Trash-ICRA19                 5,700     5,700     1     STRONG Single class
DUO                          1,782     1,946     4     VIABLE 
FishNet                     94,532    94,532     1     STRONG Single class
SUIM                           100       214     7   MARGINAL 
TrashCan                     1,200     1,803     4     VIABLE 
UIIS                           300       661     7   MARGINAL 
USIS10K                        932     1,519     7     VIABLE 

Train/Val/Test splits (70/15/15):
Dataset                        Total    Train    Val   Test  MinTrain/Cls
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Northern Adriatic Reefs            7        4      1      2             4 << TOO FEW
UCI ML Sonar                     211      147     31     33            70 << TOO FEW
SeabedObjects-KLSG             1,190      833    178    179            35 << TOO FEW
Marine_PULSE                     627      438     94     95           146
NKSID                          2,617    1,831    392    394           610
UATD                           9,200    6,440  1,380  1,380           420
SSS for Mine Detection         1,170      819    175    176           819
SWDD                           7,904    5,532  1,185  1,187         5,532
SubPipe                       10,030    7,021  1,504  1,505         7,021
UXO                           74,437   52,105 11,165 11,167        52,105
MDT                            2,471    1,729    370    372           329
NNSSS                             10        7      1      2             2 << TOO FEW
SASSED                           129       90     19     20            22 << TOO FEW
Seafloor Sediments           434,164  303,914 65,124 65,126        70,000
DIDSON                         1,000      700    150    150           350
AI4Shipwreck                     286      200     42     44           200
MBES-Slam                          4        2      0      2             2 << TOO FEW
BenthiCat                    950,000  665,000 142,500 142,500        28,000
RUIE-UHTS                        300      210     45     45            70 << TOO FEW
Brackish                          89       62     13     14             8 << TOO FEW
Trash-ICRA19                   5,700    3,989    855    856         3,989
DUO                            7,782    5,447  1,167  1,168         1,247
FishNet                       94,532   66,172 14,179 14,181        66,172
SUIM                           1,500    1,050    225    225            70 << TOO FEW
TrashCan                       7,212    5,048  1,081  1,083           840
UIIS                           4,628    3,239    694    695           210
USIS10K                       10,632    7,442  1,594  1,596           652

PART 3: Relevance Analysis for Synthetic Underwater 3D Models¶

Our goal is 3D Gaussian Splatting for generating synthetic underwater 3D models. This reframes what makes a dataset valuable:

Requirement Why it matters for 3DGS
Multi-view / sequential frames Need overlapping views to triangulate 3D geometry
Sensor poses (SE(3)) Must know where each image was captured
3D structure in scene Wrecks, infrastructure, pilings = great reconstruction targets
Sonar data (FLS/MSIS) SonarSplat uses imaging sonar polar format
Optical video + depth Camera-based GS as complementary approach
Scene diversity More varied scenes = better synthetic model generation

Key insight: Sunken ships and wrecks are ideal targets (rich 3D geometry for synthetic models).

In [63]:
# =============================================================================
# GS COMPATIBILITY SCORING
# =============================================================================
# Additional metadata for GS-specific features

gs_features = {
    "Northern Adriatic Reefs": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "Lago Grey": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "UCI ML Sonar": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "signal"},
    "SeabedObjects-KLSG": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "Marine_PULSE": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "NKSID": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "FLS"},
    "UATD": {"multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "FLS"},
    "SSS for Mine Detection": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "SWDD": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "SubPipe": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "UXO": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "FLS"},
    "MDT": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "FLS"},
    "NNSSS": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "SASSED": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SAS"},
    "Seafloor Sediments": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "DIDSON": {"multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": True, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "FLS"},
    "AI4Shipwreck": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "SSS"},
    "Cave Sonar": {"multiview": True, "poses": True, "depth": True, "video": True, "calibration": True, "sensor_compat": "MSIS"},
    "Aurora": {"multiview": True, "poses": True, "depth": True, "video": True, "calibration": True, "sensor_compat": "MBES+SSS"},
    "MBES-Slam": {"multiview": True, "poses": True, "depth": True, "video": True, "calibration": True, "sensor_compat": "MBES"},
    "BenthiCat": {"multiview": True, "poses": True, "depth": True, "video": True, "calibration": True, "sensor_compat": "SSS+Camera"},
    "RUIE-UHTS": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "Brackish": {"multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": True, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "Trash-ICRA19": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "DUO": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "FishNet": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "SUIM": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "TrashCan": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "UIIS": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
    "USIS10K": {"multiview": False, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": False, "calibration": False, "sensor_compat": "Camera"},
}

# Also add key optical video datasets not yet in catalog
extra_optical = [
    {"name": "DRUVA", "multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": True, "calibration": False,
     "sensor_compat": "Camera", "num_samples": 24000, "note": "20 videos, 360-deg views of artifacts. ICCV 2023."},
    {"name": "Sea-thru", "multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": True, "video": False, "calibration": True,
     "sensor_compat": "Camera", "num_samples": None, "note": "RAW images + depth maps + calibration. CVPR 2019."},
    {"name": "SQUID", "multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": True, "video": False, "calibration": True,
     "sensor_compat": "Camera", "num_samples": 57, "note": "57 RAW images + depth + calibration from 4 sites."},
    {"name": "UVE-38K", "multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": True, "calibration": False,
     "sensor_compat": "Camera", "num_samples": 38000, "note": "50 videos, 38K frames with paired references."},
    {"name": "UVEB", "multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": True, "calibration": False,
     "sensor_compat": "Camera", "num_samples": 453000, "note": "1,308 videos, 453K frames (38% 4K). CVPR 2024."},
    {"name": "SUVE", "multiview": True, "poses": False, "depth": False, "video": True, "calibration": False,
     "sensor_compat": "Camera", "num_samples": 140000, "note": "840 videos, 140K frames. Synthetic paired."},
    {"name": "SonarSplat Dataset", "multiview": True, "poses": True, "depth": True, "video": True, "calibration": True,
     "sensor_compat": "FLS_imaging", "num_samples": 2987, "note": "GOLD STANDARD. 9 scenes, SE(3) poses, 2 GT meshes. Already in workspace."},
]

def gs_score(feat):
    s = 0
    if feat['multiview'] and feat['video']: s += 30
    elif feat['multiview']: s += 15
    if feat['poses']: s += 25
    sc = feat['sensor_compat']
    if sc == 'FLS_imaging': s += 15
    elif sc == 'FLS': s += 12
    elif sc == 'MSIS': s += 10
    elif sc == 'Camera': s += 8
    elif sc in ('MBES', 'MBES+SSS'): s += 6
    elif 'SSS' in sc and 'Camera' in sc: s += 9
    elif 'SSS' in sc: s += 3
    if feat.get('calibration') and feat.get('depth'): s += 15
    elif feat.get('calibration'): s += 10
    elif feat.get('depth'): s += 8
    return min(s, 100)

# Build combined table
rows = []
for d in datasets_extended:
    f = gs_features[d['name']]
    rows.append({'name': d['name'], 'source': d['source'], 'num_samples': d['num_samples'],
                 'gs_score': gs_score(f), **f, 'has_3d': d.get('has_manmade_objects', False) or
                 any(t in d['content_tags'] for t in ['wreck','cave','infrastructure','pipeline'])})
for ex in extra_optical:
    rows.append({'name': ex['name'], 'source': 'optical' if ex['sensor_compat'] != 'FLS_imaging' else 'sonarsplat',
                 'num_samples': ex['num_samples'], 'gs_score': gs_score(ex), **{k: ex[k] for k in
                 ['multiview','poses','depth','video','calibration','sensor_compat']}, 'has_3d': True})

df_gs = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values('gs_score', ascending=False)

print("="*120)
print("3D GAUSSIAN SPLATTING COMPATIBILITY MATRIX")
print("(For generating synthetic underwater 3D models)")
print("="*120)
ck = lambda v: 'Y' if v else '-'
print(f"\n{'Name':<25} {'Source':<10} {'Score':>5} {'MV':>3} {'Pose':>5} {'Depth':>6} {'Video':>6} {'Cal':>4} {'Sensor':<12} {'Samples':>10}")
print("-"*120)
for _, r in df_gs.iterrows():
    ns = f"{r['num_samples']:>10,}" if pd.notna(r['num_samples']) else "       N/A"
    bar = '#' * (r['gs_score'] // 5) + '.' * (20 - r['gs_score'] // 5)
    print(f"{r['name']:<25} {r['source']:<10} {r['gs_score']:>5} {ck(r['multiview']):>3} {ck(r['poses']):>5} "
          f"{ck(r['depth']):>6} {ck(r['video']):>6} {ck(r['calibration']):>4} {r['sensor_compat']:<12} {ns}  [{bar}]")

========================================================================================================================
3D GAUSSIAN SPLATTING COMPATIBILITY MATRIX
(For generating synthetic underwater 3D models)
========================================================================================================================

Name                      Source     Score  MV  Pose  Depth  Video  Cal Sensor          Samples
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SonarSplat Dataset        sonarsplat    85   Y     Y      Y      Y    Y FLS_imaging     2,987.0  [#################...]
Cave Sonar                sonar         80   Y     Y      Y      Y    Y MSIS                N/A  [################....]
BenthiCat                 sonar         79   Y     Y      Y      Y    Y SSS+Camera    950,000.0  [###############.....]
Aurora                    sonar         76   Y     Y      Y      Y    Y MBES+SSS            N/A  [###############.....]
MBES-Slam                 sonar         76   Y     Y      Y      Y    Y MBES                4.0  [###############.....]
DIDSON                    sonar         42   Y     -      -      Y    - FLS             1,000.0  [########............]
UVEB                      optical       38   Y     -      -      Y    - Camera        453,000.0  [#######.............]
SQUID                     optical       38   Y     -      Y      -    Y Camera             57.0  [#######.............]
Sea-thru                  optical       38   Y     -      Y      -    Y Camera              N/A  [#######.............]
SUVE                      optical       38   Y     -      -      Y    - Camera        140,000.0  [#######.............]
DRUVA                     optical       38   Y     -      -      Y    - Camera         24,000.0  [#######.............]
Brackish                  optical       38   Y     -      -      Y    - Camera             89.0  [#######.............]
UVE-38K                   optical       38   Y     -      -      Y    - Camera         38,000.0  [#######.............]
UATD                      sonar         27   Y     -      -      -    - FLS             9,200.0  [#####...............]
UXO                       sonar         12   -     -      -      -    - FLS            74,437.0  [##..................]
MDT                       sonar         12   -     -      -      -    - FLS             2,471.0  [##..................]
NKSID                     sonar         12   -     -      -      -    - FLS             2,617.0  [##..................]
DUO                       optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera          7,782.0  [#...................]
UIIS                      optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera          4,628.0  [#...................]
SUIM                      optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera          1,500.0  [#...................]
TrashCan                  optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera          7,212.0  [#...................]
USIS10K                   optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera         10,632.0  [#...................]
Trash-ICRA19              optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera          5,700.0  [#...................]
RUIE-UHTS                 optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera            300.0  [#...................]
FishNet                   optical        8   -     -      -      -    - Camera         94,532.0  [#...................]
Northern Adriatic Reefs   sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS                 7.0  [....................]
Marine_PULSE              sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS               627.0  [....................]
SeabedObjects-KLSG        sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS             1,190.0  [....................]
Lago Grey                 sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS                 N/A  [....................]
SubPipe                   sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS            10,030.0  [....................]
SWDD                      sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS             7,904.0  [....................]
Seafloor Sediments        sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS           434,164.0  [....................]
AI4Shipwreck              sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS               286.0  [....................]
NNSSS                     sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS                10.0  [....................]
SSS for Mine Detection    sonar          3   -     -      -      -    - SSS             1,170.0  [....................]

SASSED                    sonar          0   -     -      -      -    - SAS               129.0  [....................]
In [64]:
# GS compatibility visualization
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(18, 14))

ax = axes[0, 0]
colors = ['#27ae60' if s >= 70 else '#f39c12' if s >= 40 else '#e74c3c' if s >= 15 else '#95a5a6'
          for s in df_gs['gs_score']]
ax.barh(range(len(df_gs)), df_gs['gs_score'], color=colors)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(df_gs)))
ax.set_yticklabels([f"{r['name']} [{r['source'][0].upper()}]" for _, r in df_gs.iterrows()], fontsize=7)
ax.set_xlabel('GS Compatibility Score (0-100)')
ax.set_title('Dataset Ranking for Synthetic 3D Model Generation', fontweight='bold')
ax.axvline(x=70, color='green', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='Ready (70+)')
ax.axvline(x=40, color='orange', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='Feasible (40+)')
ax.legend(fontsize=8)

ax = axes[0, 1]
feat_cols = ['multiview', 'poses', 'depth', 'video', 'calibration']
feat_labels = ['Multi-view', 'Poses', 'Depth', 'Video', 'Calibration']
hm = df_gs[feat_cols].astype(int).values
im = ax.imshow(hm, cmap='RdYlGn', aspect='auto', vmin=0, vmax=1)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(df_gs))); ax.set_yticklabels(df_gs['name'], fontsize=7)
ax.set_xticks(range(len(feat_labels))); ax.set_xticklabels(feat_labels, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax.set_title('GS Feature Availability', fontweight='bold')
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, shrink=0.5)

ax = axes[1, 0]
mc = df_gs['sensor_compat'].value_counts()
gs_colors = {'FLS_imaging': 'gold', 'FLS': '#27ae60', 'MSIS': '#2ecc71', 'Camera': '#3498db',
             'MBES': '#9b59b6', 'MBES+SSS': '#8e44ad', 'SSS+Camera': '#1abc9c'}
ax.barh(range(len(mc)), mc.values, color=[gs_colors.get(m, '#e74c3c') for m in mc.index])
ax.set_yticks(range(len(mc))); ax.set_yticklabels(mc.index)
ax.set_xlabel('Datasets'); ax.set_title('Sensor Modality (Green=GS-friendly)', fontweight='bold')

ax = axes[1, 1]
tiers = {'Ready (70+)': df_gs[df_gs['gs_score'] >= 70], 'Feasible (40-69)': df_gs[(df_gs['gs_score'] >= 40) & (df_gs['gs_score'] < 70)],
         'Limited (15-39)': df_gs[(df_gs['gs_score'] >= 15) & (df_gs['gs_score'] < 40)], 'Incompatible (<15)': df_gs[df_gs['gs_score'] < 15]}
tier_colors = ['#27ae60', '#f39c12', '#e67e22', '#e74c3c']
for (label, tdf), color in zip(tiers.items(), tier_colors):
    total = tdf['num_samples'].dropna().sum()
    ax.bar(label, total, color=color, alpha=0.8)
    ax.text(label, total * 1.3 if total > 0 else 1, f'{total:,.0f}', ha='center', fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel('Total Samples'); ax.set_title('Data Pool by GS Readiness', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_yscale('symlog', linthresh=100)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

18. Final Verdict: Dataset Tier List for Synthetic Underwater 3D Models¶

In [65]:
print("="*100)
print("DATASET TIER LIST FOR SYNTHETIC UNDERWATER 3D MODEL GENERATION")
print("="*100)

print("""
=== TIER S: READY FOR GAUSSIAN SPLATTING (multi-view + poses + calibration) ===

  1. SonarSplat Dataset (2,987 FLS frames, 9 scenes, SE(3) poses, 2 GT meshes)
     ALREADY IN WORKSPACE. Gold standard for sonar-based GS.
     Includes shipwreck (monohansett_3D) + infrastructure + pilings.

  2. Cave Sonar (MSIS+Multi, ROSBAG with SLAM navigation)
     Full poses + depth from SLAM. Rich 3D cave geometry.
     Needs: MSIS -> SonarSplat format adaptation.

  3. Aurora (MBES+SSS+Multi, full navigation)
     Multi-beam depth + SLAM poses. Large-scale seabed mapping.

  4. MBES-Slam (MBES, ROSBAG, 4 sequences with full nav)
     Small but complete: poses + depth + calibration.

  5. BenthiCat (950K tiles, SSS+Camera, AUV navigation)
     Has BOTH sonar and camera with poses. Flat seabed BUT
     opti-acoustic tools can extract camera sequences with poses.

=== TIER A: HIGH POTENTIAL (video = multi-view, needs COLMAP for poses) ===

  6. DRUVA (20 videos, 360-deg views of underwater artifacts)
     BEST for optical GS: 360-degree coverage = ideal reconstruction input.
     Workflow: extract frames -> COLMAP SfM -> 3DGS.

  7. Sea-thru (RAW images + depth maps + calibration)
     Depth maps enable depth-supervised GS. Calibration included.

  8. UVEB (1,308 videos, 453K frames, 38% 4K) - Largest UW video dataset.
  9. SUVE (840 videos, 140K frames) - Large synthetic-paired video.
  10. UVE-38K (50 videos, 38K frames) - Good quality + paired refs.
  11. SQUID (57 images + depth + calibration from 4 sites)

=== TIER B: USEFUL REFERENCE DATA (single images, no poses) ===

  FLS datasets (right sensor but no multi-view/poses):
    UATD (9,200), UXO (74,437), NKSID (2,617), MDT (2,471)

  Wreck reference images:
    SeabedObjects-KLSG (385 wreck images), AI4Shipwreck (286)

  Mixed optical datasets:
    SUIM, UIIS, USIS10K (contain some wrecks/objects)

=== TIER C: NOT USEFUL FOR 3D MODEL GENERATION ===

  Flat seabed texture datasets: BenthiCat classes, Seafloor Sediments,
    NNSSS, SASSED (no 3D structure to reconstruct)

  Fish/biological only: FishNet, DIDSON, DUO, Brackish (dynamic subjects)

  Signal data: UCI ML Sonar (frequency domain, not images)
""")

print("="*100)
print("QUANTIFIED DATA POOL")
print("="*100)
tier_s = {"SonarSplat": 2987, "Cave Sonar": "N/A", "Aurora": "N/A", "MBES-Slam": 4, "BenthiCat": 950000}
tier_a = {"DRUVA": 24000, "Sea-thru": "N/A", "UVEB": 453000, "SUVE": 140000, "UVE-38K": 38000, "SQUID": 57}

print("\n  Tier S (ready for GS):")
for n, v in tier_s.items():
    print(f"    {n:<20} {v if isinstance(v,int) else v:>10} frames")
print("\n  Tier A (video, needs COLMAP):")
for n, v in tier_a.items():
    print(f"    {n:<20} {v if isinstance(v,int) else v:>10} frames")
total_a = sum(v for v in tier_a.values() if isinstance(v, int))
print(f"\n  Total Tier A optical frames: {total_a:,}")
print(f"  These can be processed: Video -> COLMAP SfM -> 3DGS -> Synthetic 3D Models")

====================================================================================================
DATASET TIER LIST FOR SYNTHETIC UNDERWATER 3D MODEL GENERATION
====================================================================================================

=== TIER S: READY FOR GAUSSIAN SPLATTING (multi-view + poses + calibration) ===

  1. SonarSplat Dataset (2,987 FLS frames, 9 scenes, SE(3) poses, 2 GT meshes)
     ALREADY IN WORKSPACE. Gold standard for sonar-based GS.
     Includes shipwreck (monohansett_3D) + infrastructure + pilings.

  2. Cave Sonar (MSIS+Multi, ROSBAG with SLAM navigation)
     Full poses + depth from SLAM. Rich 3D cave geometry.
     Needs: MSIS -> SonarSplat format adaptation.

  3. Aurora (MBES+SSS+Multi, full navigation)
     Multi-beam depth + SLAM poses. Large-scale seabed mapping.

  4. MBES-Slam (MBES, ROSBAG, 4 sequences with full nav)
     Small but complete: poses + depth + calibration.

  5. BenthiCat (950K tiles, SSS+Camera, AUV navigation)
     Has BOTH sonar and camera with poses. Flat seabed BUT
     opti-acoustic tools can extract camera sequences with poses.

=== TIER A: HIGH POTENTIAL (video = multi-view, needs COLMAP for poses) ===

  6. DRUVA (20 videos, 360-deg views of underwater artifacts)
     BEST for optical GS: 360-degree coverage = ideal reconstruction input.
     Workflow: extract frames -> COLMAP SfM -> 3DGS.

  7. Sea-thru (RAW images + depth maps + calibration)
     Depth maps enable depth-supervised GS. Calibration included.

  8. UVEB (1,308 videos, 453K frames, 38% 4K) - Largest UW video dataset.
  9. SUVE (840 videos, 140K frames) - Large synthetic-paired video.
  10. UVE-38K (50 videos, 38K frames) - Good quality + paired refs.
  11. SQUID (57 images + depth + calibration from 4 sites)

=== TIER B: USEFUL REFERENCE DATA (single images, no poses) ===

  FLS datasets (right sensor but no multi-view/poses):
    UATD (9,200), UXO (74,437), NKSID (2,617), MDT (2,471)

  Wreck reference images:
    SeabedObjects-KLSG (385 wreck images), AI4Shipwreck (286)

  Mixed optical datasets:
    SUIM, UIIS, USIS10K (contain some wrecks/objects)

=== TIER C: NOT USEFUL FOR 3D MODEL GENERATION ===

  Flat seabed texture datasets: BenthiCat classes, Seafloor Sediments,
    NNSSS, SASSED (no 3D structure to reconstruct)

  Fish/biological only: FishNet, DIDSON, DUO, Brackish (dynamic subjects)

  Signal data: UCI ML Sonar (frequency domain, not images)

====================================================================================================
QUANTIFIED DATA POOL
====================================================================================================

  Tier S (ready for GS):
    SonarSplat                 2987 frames
    Cave Sonar                  N/A frames
    Aurora                      N/A frames
    MBES-Slam                     4 frames
    BenthiCat                950000 frames

  Tier A (video, needs COLMAP):
    DRUVA                     24000 frames
    Sea-thru                    N/A frames
    UVEB                     453000 frames
    SUVE                     140000 frames
    UVE-38K                   38000 frames
    SQUID                        57 frames

  Total Tier A optical frames: 655,057
  These can be processed: Video -> COLMAP SfM -> 3DGS -> Synthetic 3D Models
